title: NVIDIA GB200 and GB300: Liquid-cooled for AI efficiency | Benjamin Chan posted on the topic | LinkedIn
text:
NEWS: Liquid-cooled NVIDIA GB200 and GB300 NVL72 systems are made to power trillion-parameter models — with a fraction of the energy and water use. 📉Up to 25x more cost-efficient 💧Over 300x greater water savings ⚡30x the performance Learn more.
NVIDIA GB200 and GB300: Liquid-cooled for AI efficiency
More Relevant Posts
- 
                
      The Future is Now: Why 800 VDC Will Redefine Data Centers It feels like the 1890s again—back then, AC vs. DC shaped the future of electricity. Today, we face a similar turning point in data centers. AI platforms like NVIDIA Rubin Ultra demand up to 1 MW per rack. Traditional 400V AC or 48V DC? They simply can’t keep up. The answer: 800 VDC architecture. Why it matters: ✅ Smaller cables & reduced copper costs ✅ Higher efficiency & fewer conversion losses ✅ Simplified maintenance & improved reliability At GTC 2025, NVIDIA showcased an 800 VDC PSU powering 576 GPUs in a single rack. Schneider Electric is ready—with solutions available well before 2027. The future isn’t coming—it’s already here. Are you ready? 👉 Read the full story: https://lnkd.in/daaMmnG7 To view or add a comment, sign in 
- 
                
      Deploying NVIDIA’s GB200 NVL72 in secure environments takes more than compute. It requires precision across power, cooling, and compliance. At NVIDIA GTC DC, Vertiv will share a lightning talk on the infrastructure choices that enable liquid-cooled, high-density AI systems. Discover how direct-to-chip liquid cooling and advanced electrical distribution are engineered for NVIDIA’s GB200 NVL72 thermal requirements, accelerating mission-critical workloads while meeting strict regulatory standards. Don’t miss Vertiv’s lightning talk: http://ms.spr.ly/6048s75b4 To view or add a comment, sign in 
- 
                
      The rule that just got rewritten for years... Data center operators previously lived by one golden rule: "Keep liquid out of the white space". AI changed that. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling now brings coolant into the server onto the GPUs, memory, and CPUs because air cooling can no longer handle the thermal load. This isn’t a small change. It’s a full rewrite of how data centers operate. It demands: • End-to-end coolant management • New commissioning workflows • Real-time leak detection • Redefined safety protocols • And much, much more. And your teams need to be trained for it; hands-on, lab-based, and built around live systems. That’s exactly what Salute delivers. Explore how we’re preparing the industry for what’s next: https://lnkd.in/eVHKKa6B #LiquidCooling #AIDataCenters #DirectToChip #DataCenterInnovation #SaluteAI To view or add a comment, sign in 
- 
                
      We couldn’t be more excited about the arrival of our 1st PoliCloud to USA 🇺🇸. More precisely to Austin, TX. 😊 In the hands of Data Factory, this ultra performant 40 foot container will be making waves in the world of AI workloads with its 360 GPU capacity delivering 37.7 PetaFLOPS in FP32 operations! 🚀 Yes, you read this right, take a breath and now imagine the power it delivers, all without a drop of water and less than 0.5 MWh… All of that in less than 100 sqm… built and delivered in less than 6 months. 🌎 Our goal is to build super-flexible power farms, the next-generation infrastructure designed to balance power grids and deliver high-performance computing. Our mission is to unite energy and computing to create long lasting value for the grid, our customers, and our investors. The arrival yesterday of this first PoliCloud at a Data Factory site is a small step for us—and a giant leap for the HPC industry. From this point forward, we’re expanding beyond ASICs by deploying GPUs on site to power AI workloads with grid-friendly compute. This milestone reflects months of work alongside our amazing partners, PoliCloud and Hivenet. Huge thanks to every crew on the ground 🫡 Now, let's light up some GPUs! ⚡️ #Energy #Compute #HPC #AI #DataCenters PoliCloud Hivenet To view or add a comment, sign in

      Today we are proud to reinforce our commitment to supporting the industry’s transition to 800 VDC power architectures in support of NVIDIA’s next generation GPU’s, which are a critical requirement for emerging high-density rack systems being adopted across next generation #data_centers. “The move to 800 VDC is a natural evolution as compute density increases, and Schneider Electric is committed to helping customers make that transition safely and reliably,” said Jim Simonelli, CTO, Data Centers, Schneider Electric. “Our expertise lies in understanding the full power ecosystem, from grid to server, and designing solutions that integrate seamlessly, perform predictably, and operate safely.” For more on this announcement, click here: http://spr.ly/6043AIV8b #DataCentersoftheFuture #Power To view or add a comment, sign in
